items.briefing:
Google has flagged a flaw in a tool called KnowledgeDeliver, alongside a reported surge in Chinese-linked phishing activity — both worth being aware of as phishing remains one of the most common ways Australian organisations are compromised. Separately, a malware campaign dubbed "Megalodon" has infected thousands of GitHub repositories, posing a risk to anyone downloading or using open-source code from that platform.

Reports out today also highlight that many Australian executives are leaving AI decisions to IT alone, which can create security blind spots when AI tools are adopted without proper oversight. If your organisation uses AI or cloud-based software, make sure someone beyond IT is asking questions about how data is being handled.

**Practical tip:** Be cautious when downloading code or tools from GitHub — even popular repositories can be compromised, so verify sources before use.

featured.title: Kinetic IT report flags AI & sovereign execution gap
featured.quote: Australian agencies lack funding and skills to safely deploy AI systems securely.
